name: White and Dark Chocolate Dogwood Bark (Marcel
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 325°F.
2. Toast pecans on a baking sheet in the preheated oven for 8 minutes. Remove and set aside to cool at room temperature.
3. Heat 1 inch of water in the bottom half of a double boiler over medium heat.
4. Place white chocolate in the top half of the double boiler. Stir until melted and smooth (about 4 minutes). Transfer to a 1-quart bowl and set aside.
5. Thoroughly wash and dry the top half of the double boiler. Heat water in the bottom half over medium heat.
6. Place semisweet chocolate in the top half. Stir until melted and smooth (about 6 minutes). Transfer to a 4-quart bowl. Allow to stand at room temperature for 5 minutes.
7. Add pecans to the bowl of melted semisweet chocolate. Fold until combined.
8. Pour the chocolate pecan mixture onto a nonstick baking sheet with sides. Use a rubber spatula to spread evenly.
9. Drizzle white chocolate, one tablespoon at a time, over the surface. Spread and blend into a marbleized effect (avoid overmixing).
10. Allow to cool at room temperature for 30 minutes. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate until hard (about 1 hour).
11. Remove from refrigerator and transfer to a cutting board. Cut into irregular pieces. Store in a tightly sealed container until ready to use.
